documents.title: Reliability Prediction of Electronic Equipment
documents.relationship: Controlled governing edition for Perdura's MIL-HDBK-217F calculations.
findings: Appendix C contains 38 numbered references.
The scan contains several document-number and accession transcription defects recorded on the affected Appendix C entries.
limitations: Handbook transcription parity is not empirical validation for a specific design.

documents.title: Reliability Prediction MIL-HDBK-217 Subsidiary Specification
documents.relationship: Optional calculation-affecting subsidiary rules activated by the A/V51.1 selection.
limitations: Referenced VITA 51.0, VITA 51.2, and RAC methods remain separate sources.

documents.title: Reliability Prediction of Electronic Equipment
documents.relationship: Base Revision F scan used to distinguish content superseded by Notice 2.
limitations: Not the governing Notice 2 edition.

documents.title: Environmental Test Methods for Microcircuits, Part 1: Test Methods 1000-1999
documents.relationship: Later method-volume context for microcircuit screening and environmental tests.
findings: Part of the five-volume 2019 baseline that superseded MIL-STD-883K Change 3.
limitations: Does not establish parity with the MIL-STD-883 revision contemporaneous with the 1995 handbook.

documents.title: Mechanical Test Methods for Microcircuits, Part 2: Test Methods 2000-2999

findings: Part of the five-volume 2019 baseline that superseded MIL-STD-883K Change 3.
limitations: Does not establish parity with the MIL-STD-883 revision contemporaneous with the 1995 handbook.

documents.title: Electrical Tests (Digital) for Microcircuits, Part 3: Test Methods 3000-3999
documents.relationship: Later method-volume context for digital electrical tests.
findings: Part of the five-volume 2019 baseline that superseded MIL-STD-883K Change 3.
limitations: Does not establish parity with the MIL-STD-883 revision contemporaneous with the 1995 handbook.

documents.title: Electrical Tests (Linear) for Microcircuits, Part 4: Test Methods 4000-4999
documents.relationship: Later method-volume context for linear electrical tests.

documents.title: Test Procedures for Microcircuits, Part 5: Test Methods 5000-5999
documents.relationship: Later method-volume context for microcircuit procedures.

documents.title: Microcircuits
documents.relationship: Current-edition context used only to interpret modern class terminology.
findings: Modern Classes B and S must not be used to reinterpret the handbook's B-1 screening bucket.
limitations: Restricted-source copy; metadata and findings only.
Thirty years later than the governing handbook edition.

documents.title: Electronic Reliability Design Handbook
documents.relationship: Guidance-only cross-check for reliability modeling and service-life interpretation.
findings: The handbook explicitly identifies itself as guidance and not a requirement.
limitations: Not an Appendix C derivation source.

documents.title: Reliability/Maintainability/Testability Design for Dormancy
documents.relationship: Later dormancy-design context; not the selected nonoperating rate model.
limitations: Not listed in MIL-HDBK-217F Appendix C.

documents.title: RADC Reliability Engineer's Toolkit
findings: This report is a toolkit, not the VHSIC report associated with ADA214601 in Appendix C entry 30.
limitations: Summary material cannot replace primary model reports.

documents.title: RADC Reliability Engineer's Toolkit
documents.relationship: Earlier application-oriented Toolkit issue and historical derating-lineage cross-check; distinct from the later RADC-TR-89-171 report.
findings: Topic D1, printed pages 41-45, presents summary part-stress-derating guidance and directs the reader to AFSC Pamphlet 800-27 rather than establishing an independent complete rulebook.
limitations: Only the derating-lineage context was reviewed; the Toolkit summary cannot replace the cited primary rulebook.

documents.document_number: Rome Laboratory Reliability Engineer's Toolkit (April 1993 issue)
documents.title: Rome Laboratory Reliability Engineer's Toolkit
documents.relationship: Updated successor to the July 1988 RADC Toolkit; Topic D1 provides a level-selection rubric and summary derating tables but is not silently substituted for any enabled primary-source profile.
findings: Topic D1, printed pages 37 onward, supplies a five-factor score for manual Level I/II/III selection and summary technology-specific derating rows.
The 1993 issue explicitly lists RL-TR-92-11 as advanced-technology derating background and describes itself as an update to the July 1988 Toolkit.
limitations: Topic D1 is summary guidance rather than a promulgated military standard and does not erase primary-source applicability, exceptions, or qualification requirements.
Only the derating topic and identity/lineage material were reviewed for this audit.

documents.title: NASA Standard Electrical, Electronic, and Electromechanical (EEE) Parts List
documents.relationship: Historical source for the separately labeled MIL-STD-975M Appendix A derating profile; Notices 1 and 2 do not alter Appendix A and Notice 3 cancels the standard without replacement.
findings: Appendix A contains one historical rule set across 16 commodity sections; it is not a three-level method.
Notices 1 and 2 change parts listings but no Appendix A criterion.
Notice 3 canceled the standard without replacement on 5 May 1998.
Appendix A.24 delegates pulse and irregular-waveform resistor applications; the applicable reviewed MIL-HDBK-978B Volume I sections are recorded on Appendix C entry 35.
limitations: Historical and canceled; not current NASA practice.
Qualitative, approximate, irregular-waveform, and manufacturer-specific pulse judgments remain not evaluated rather than being reconstructed as numerical limits.

documents.title: Reliability Derating Procedures
documents.relationship: Historical contractor technical report and proposed military-standard framework for advanced-device derating; not a promulgated standard and not a replacement for established-part sources.
findings: Tables 1-9 provide executable three-level advanced-device limits.
Table 10 prints ambiguous dB power units and leaves exactly 500 MHz unassigned.
Several thermal equations contain dimensional or typographical defects and the internal model is described as a ballpark estimate.
limitations: The report depends on AFSC Pamphlet 800-27 and RADC-TR-82-177 for established parts and constituent hybrid devices.
The detailed thermal models are not suitable for an acceptance decision without independent correction and validation.

documents.title: Advanced Technology Component Derating
documents.relationship: Historical advanced-technology extension to AFSC Pamphlet 800-27; retained as a separately named technical-report profile rather than merged into NASA, Rome Toolkit, or RADC-TR-84-254 values.
findings: The report extends the AFSC 800-27 framework for ASIC/VHSIC, memories, microwave, power-semiconductor, optoelectronic, passive, and SAW technologies.

The report p. 134 MIL-STD-198E capacitor cross-reference was resolved against the reviewed base and Notices 1-3; its hard AC-plus-DC rule remains an RL criterion, while the cross-referenced nonmandatory application precautions are tracked as granular coverage advisories.
limitations: Applicability and overlap with the underlying AFSC 800-27 rulebook must remain explicit.
No result may imply current military-standard or NASA conformance.

documents.title: Capacitors, Selection and Use of
documents.relationship: Primary cross-reference for the chip-capacitor application precautions cited by RL-TR-92-11 report p. 134; canceled by Notice 3 on 14 July 1999 and superseded by guidance-only MIL-HDBK-198.
findings: Reviewed official DLA base SHA-256 ab159381fbc2cecf249088252ca9e5d8c5afb88b00f7fdfee9810d15d6192a77, Notice 1 SHA-256 a2631090385bd78f3f7a9a7c22c571158ecf48240d39fed5d1417daeac6c3d0d, and Notice 2 SHA-256 789b3ad07f521a60d2d9180a1d5fd553784c6697c2abbea83217d658aa358642; reviewed static Notice 3 archive SHA-256 9a8a838d8a90f169596fe936d3e37239b182788cfc6815b25188e40f9c57f13a.
The foreword states that application information and performance characteristics are guidance and are not mandatory.

Section 6.5 addresses peak voltage including transients, charge/discharge current and time constant, internal heating and ambient, environment, insulation resistance, and special ratings/tests for AC or pulse service.
Section 703.1 describes CWR use with small AC relative to DC and supplemental moisture protection; its approximate 55%-at-125 C point is dominated by RL-TR-92-11's stricter 85 C and 60% limits.
Section 903.1 as replaced by Notice 2 addresses ceramic dielectric variation, humidity/contamination, pure-silver migration under humidity plus DC, and substrate thermal-expansion compatibility.

Notice 1 changes only non-chip CSR section 701; Notice 2 changes CDR styles and surface-mount scope without materially changing the relevant precautions; Notice 3 cancels the standard and preserves its information in MIL-HDBK-198.
limitations: Technical review was limited to the foreword, sections 6.5, 703.1, and 903.1, plus notice effects relevant to RL-TR-92-11 chip capacitors.
The dynamically stamped DLA downloads and static Notice 3 archive are metadata-only and are not stored in the repository.
Underlying detailed ratings and qualification requirements remain in the applicable MIL-C-55365 and MIL-C-55681 issues and specification sheets.

The report states that its models exclude satellite applications.

appendix_c_entries.title: NASA Parts Application Handbook
appendix_c_entries.relationship: Appendix C reference 35 and the explicit MIL-STD-975M Appendix A.24 delegation for pulse and irregular-waveform resistor applications.
supports: resistor pulse and intermittent-load application guidance
findings: Reviewed official NASA copy SHA-256 7ad4d29529fa42b24676fc3e22f178c2d2c099617c15fab15af8db536aa453be.
Section 3.1.6.2, printed 3-12–3-13, requires maximum-voltage, continuous-overpower-fault, average-power, and steep-wavefront review for pulse/intermittent duties.
Section 3.2.5.2, printed 3-23–3-24, limits low-duty RCR peak voltage to 2× RCWV and states an approximate 30–40× normal-power caution rather than a hard acceptance boundary.
Section 3.3.5.3, printed 3-31, limits low-duty established-reliability fixed-film peak voltage to 1.4× RCWV and peak power to 4× maximum rating.
limitations: Technical review for this implementation is limited to Volume I resistor sections 3.1.6.2, 3.2.5.2, and 3.3.5.3; the five-volume series was not comprehensively reviewed.
The reviewed PDF is metadata-only and is not stored in the repository.
The handbook supplies qualitative or manufacturer-dependent duties for irregular, high-duty, appreciable-width, and other-construction cases; Perdura leaves those judgments not evaluated.
